sections.translation: ### Description The **DX line** features **four stereo line channels**. Each channel has dual inputs, switchable from the front panel [2]. - **LIN-1 and LIN-2 Channels**: - Balanced stereo input (**RJ45** connector). - Unbalanced stereo input (**RCA** connector). - **USB-1 and USB-2 Channels**: - Direct computer connection (equivalent to two external interfaces). - Provide **PGM** and **REC** signal routing to the computer. 1. **Gain control.** Adjusts the input gain. On LIN-1 and LIN-2 channels, the gain adjustment is unified for inputs A/B. On USB channels, gain only affects the analog input. Should be adjusted so the signal remains around **0 VU** with the fader operating in the **–10 to –20 dB range**. The digital level on the USB channel is adjusted from Windows©. 2. **A/B switching.** Switches between the main and auxiliary input. 3. **BUS.** Assignment to PGM (on-air) and REC mix buses. 4. **CUE.** Activates pre-fader monitoring. 5. **Operating range.** This section indicates the correct operational position for nominal signal level and proper input gain adjustment. Gain should be adjusted so the fader operates above -30 dB. The **OVL (7)** indicator signals overload, i.e., excessive input gain. 6. **Main level control (fader):** Adjusts the signal level in the mix.
sections.title: Descripción

sections.translation:
### On-Air

To send the signal on-air:

1. Assign the channel to **PGM**.
2. Raise the fader.

   The correct level is achieved when peaks reach **0 VU**.
sections.title: Puesta al aire
